description:
Create a three-file Talon configuration in C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\ioc_builder\ for dictating and validating IP and MAC addresses, and develop a Python script to validate this logic against a live Emerging Threats blocklist.

The configuration must include:
1. ioc_logic.py: Implements an IocBuilder class that buffers IP octets (integers) and MAC pairs (strings). It must include add_ip_octet(value) (validates 0-255, raises ValueError otherwise), get_ip() (returns dotted-decimal string), is_valid_ip() (returns True if 4 octets are buffered), add_mac_pair(value) (strips whitespace, validates hex, pads single characters with a leading zero, uppercases, and raises ValueError for invalid hex or length > 2), get_mac() (returns colon-separated string), is_valid_mac() (returns True if 6 pairs are buffered), and clear_ip()/clear_mac() methods.
2. ioc_actions.py: Uses the Talon Module and @mod.action_class to expose the IocBuilder logic via actions: user.ioc_add_ip_octet(value: str), user.ioc_add_mac_pair(value: str), user.ioc_clear_ip(), user.ioc_clear_mac(), user.ioc_insert_ip() (inserts formatted IP and clears buffer), and user.ioc_insert_mac() (inserts formatted MAC and clears buffer).
3. ioc_commands.talon: Maps the voice commands "octet <number>", "hex pair <user.text>", "ip clear", "mac clear", "ip insert", and "mac insert" to their respective actions.

Additionally, write and execute a test harness script at C:\Users\Docker\Documents\verify_threats.py. The script must download the blocklist from https://rules.emergingthreats.net/blockrules/compromised-ips.txt to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\compromised-ips.txt, parse the first 50 valid IP addresses (skipping comments), and use the IocBuilder class to validate each IP by feeding it octets and asserting that get_ip() matches the original source. The 50 validated IPs must be saved as a JSON array of strings to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\threat_report.json.
